scoring.weighting: Multiply score by weight. Do not use totals to override a failure on a non-negotiable criterion.

criteria.title: Outcome fit
questions: Does the direction make the primary task and decision hierarchy obvious?
Does it support the audience's context and expected trust level?

criteria.title: Content fit
questions: Does the composition express actual content relationships?
Does it survive representative long, empty, error, and localized content?

criteria.title: Distinctiveness
questions: Are two or three memorable signals specific to this product?
Would a competitor substitution test reveal generic copy or styling?

criteria.title: System coherence
questions: Can type, color, geometry, imagery, and motion be expressed as repeatable rules?

criteria.title: Accessibility
questions: Do contrast, focus, keyboard, reading order, target, and reduced-motion requirements fit without fighting the direction?
Are color, motion, and shape redundant rather than exclusive cues?

criteria.title: Responsive resilience
questions: Does hierarchy survive reflow instead of relying on one composition?
Do difficult widths, large text, RTL, and mixed input remain usable?

criteria.title: Performance feasibility
questions: Can the direction meet route budgets on representative hardware?
Are fonts, images, effects, and motion scoped to their value?

criteria.title: Implementation and adoption
questions: Can the direction be tokenized and composed without excessive one-offs?
Is migration or delivery risk proportionate to outcome value?

criteria.title: Evidence and integrity
questions: Are reference-derived and assumed decisions separated?
Are proof, claims, assets, and content verified?
Have template-like anti-patterns been justified or removed?

comparison_record.combination_rule: Combine only named compatible decisions, never average complete directions into a stylistic compromise.
